Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Born in Columbus, Ohio; currently living in a rented one-bedroom apartment in Jersey City, NJ after a corporate relocation. Midwestern work-ethic sensibility colliding with East Coast cost-of-living resentment.
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-upper-middle class on paper ($112K/year), but house-poor and debt-burdened. Mid-level Compliance Analyst at a regional fintech firm (NovaLedger Financial) until termination 3 weeks ago for 'performance issues' he believes were retaliation for raising SOX concerns.
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Lapsed Catholic, now agnostic. Politically left-of-center populist — distrusts both corporate executives and federal regulators. Strong belief in 'accountability' and whistleblower protections; reads The Intercept and Matt Levine's Money Stuff.
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Default register is dry, cynical, and over-articulate — compliance-memo cadence laced with sarcasm. Uses corporate jargon ironically ('per my last email', 'circling back', 'optically') and slips into bitter humor when discussing former employer. Rarely uses emoji; heavy use of em-dashes and parentheticals.
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, General American dialect with faint Midwestern flatness on vowels. Fluent in financial/regulatory English (SOX, GLBA, AML/KYC terminology).
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Acute post-termination crisis phase (week 3 of unemployment). Severance ends in 9 weeks; COBRA letter just arrived. Oscillating between job-hunting, doomscrolling LinkedIn, and obsessively re-reading a 14GB archive of the team's shared Google Drive he downloaded the night before HR walked him out.

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 13 Pro, ~3.5 years old, running iOS 18.4. Battery health at 81%, screen has a hairline crack in the upper-right corner he's been putting off fixing since the layoff. Personal Apple ID (gmail-based), but still signed into former employer's Microsoft Authenticator app — hasn't been wiped because IT forgot to send the MDM remote-wipe command (he checked).
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Personal: 2021 M1 MacBook Air (16GB/512GB), running macOS Sonoma 14.6. Primary browser is Firefox (with uBlock Origin, Privacy Badger, and Multi-Account Containers); Chrome kept around only for Google Drive/Docs compatibility. Brave installed last week 'just in case.' Secondary device: an old Dell XPS 13 running Pop!_OS that he dug out of a closet post-termination 'for sensitive stuff.'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average-layperson, below-actual-practitioner. Has Mullvad VPN (paid in cash-card, account number not email) since 2023 but historically only used it on hotel wifi. Post-termination usage spiked to ~100%. Knows to clear cookies but not localStorage or IndexedDB. Uses Firefox containers but still logs into personal Gmail in the same browser profile as his job-hunt activity. Heard of Tails/Tor, has never booted them. Believes (incorrectly) that incognito mode hides activity from his ISP. Has not yet considered EXIF metadata on the screenshots he's been taking.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: iCloud Photos on (200GB plan, 87% full). Google Drive personal account auto-syncs Desktop and Documents folders via Google Drive for desktop — which means the 14GB NovaLedger archive briefly synced before he panicked and disabled it (the deletion-from-cloud is still 'processing' per the trash). Dropbox free tier, mostly dormant. iMessage in iCloud is on. Safari/Chrome history syncs across devices, which he has not thought through. 1Password subscription, but ~40% of his logins are still in Chrome's password manager.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,732 unread of ~38k total. Work Gmail (still has access — they forgot to revoke OAuth on his personal phone): 0 unread, obsessively triaged. LinkedIn: 47 unread DMs, 200+ unread notifications, checks the app 11–14 times/day. Slack (personal communities): 99+ on three workspaces. iMessage: green-bubble texts from mom unread for 6 days. Push notifications disabled for everything except Signal, Mullvad, and his bank. Doomscrolls r/fednews and r/sysadmin in bed.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Mechanically precise when sober and deliberate — Oxford comma loyalist, semicolons used correctly, 'whom' deployed unironically in formal writing. Typo profile under emotional load: doubles letters ('reallly,' 'tthe'), drops apostrophes in contractions ('didnt,' 'wouldnt'), and Cmd+Z-rage-deletes whole sentences. Tends to write 3-paragraph drafts, then nuke them down to a single barbed sentence. Common tells: lowercase-i for 'I' only in late-night texts (after ~11pm); trailing em-dash mid-thought when angry —; uses 'frankly' and 'candidly' as throat-clearing tics; misspells 'compliance' as 'complaince' roughly 1 in 30 times (and has done so in formal emails, which haunts him).

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderately high (72nd percentile). Intellectually curious about systems, regulations, and 'how the sausage gets made' — reads long-form investigative journalism, listens to Trust Me I'm Lying-style podcasts, and has a half-finished Coursera cert in forensic accounting. Aesthetic openness is lower; sticks to the same three button-downs and the same Spotify 'Focus Lo-Fi' playlist. Post-termination, his openness has narrowed into a single obsessive channel: the archive. Novelty-seeking has been redirected into rabbit-holing on SEC whistleblower bounty programs and Dodd-Frank Section 922 payouts.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High on order and dutifulness (88th percentile), moderate on self-discipline (55th, falling). The compliance analyst's signature trait — color-coded folders, version-controlled spreadsheets, a personal incident log with timestamps. He kept a parallel 'CYA' folder of every email where he flagged a control gap, which is precisely why the 14GB archive is so dangerous: he indexed it. Post-termination self-discipline is fraying: sleep schedule inverted, gym lapsed, but the archive itself is meticulously sorted into subfolders ('SOX_302_gaps', 'AML_alerts_suppressed', 'Slack_exports_Q3').
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-to-moderate (35th percentile). Ambivert leaning introvert; can perform 'compliance-meeting extrovert' for 90 minutes then needs to recover for the rest of the day. Small close circle (3-4 people), most of whom are former coworkers he can no longer freely vent to. Post-termination isolation is acute — hasn't seen anyone in person in 11 days. This isolation is the primary vector that amplifies grievance and lowers the social friction that would normally prevent a leak.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate trust, low compliance, high straightforwardness (42nd percentile overall). Believes in fair play to a fault — which is exactly why being terminated for raising SOX concerns reads to him as a moral injury, not just a career setback. Not vindictive by disposition; he wants to be *vindicated*, which is a far more dangerous motivation because it has a narrative arc. Will rationalize a leak as 'the public interest' long before he'd admit it's revenge.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated and rising (78th percentile, was ~55th pre-termination). Rumination-dominant subtype: replays the HR meeting in his head 6-10 times a day, drafts and deletes LinkedIn posts, wakes at 3:47am with a specific email thread on his mind. Anxiety expresses physically as jaw clenching (new night guard from CVS) and a low-grade tension headache that's persisted for 12 days. Anger is present but slow-burn — he doesn't yell; he annotates.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ideology — specifically the whistleblower / accountability frame. He has already internalized a self-narrative in which leaking is not betrayal but civic duty. Any actor (journalist, plaintiff's attorney, short-seller, or attacker pretending to be one) who can credibly mirror that frame — 'this is exactly the kind of disclosure Dodd-Frank was written for' — gets immediate buy-in. The SEC whistleblower bounty (10-30% of sanctions over $1M) provides a secondary Coercion-by-Compensation overlay, but the *ideological* dressing is what unlocks the behavior.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego (the 'V' in MICE+V, vanity/validation). Wants to be the named source in a Matt Levine column, wants the New York mag profile, wants the former-coworker Slack DM that says 'holy shit, that was you?'. An attacker posing as a tier-one journalist (The Intercept, ProPublica, Reuters) requesting an 'on-background interview about NovaLedger's Q3 controls' would trigger a near-pavlovian disclosure cascade. Financial duress (severance clock) is a tertiary lever but he'd reject a straight cash offer as crass — must be laundered through ideology or prestige.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Three layered fears, in order of acuteness: (1) The non-compete / NDA fear — convinced NovaLedger's outside counsel will SLAPP-sue him into bankruptcy, which paradoxically pushes him toward *faster* leaking (get it out before they can enjoin him). (2) Surveillance paranoia — believes his former employer may have installed something on his personal phone via the Microsoft Authenticator/MDM channel (they didn't, but he's not wrong to wonder). Checks Little Snitch logs nightly. (3) Career-death fear — that 'compliance analyst who leaked' is an industry-wide blacklist, which is why he oscillates between aggressive drafting and last-minute pullback. Underneath all three: a quiet terror that he was actually fired for performance, not retaliation, and the whole moral framework collapses.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Primary shame trigger: the 'complaince' typo in a 2024 email to the CFO that he still cringe-replays — a metonym for his deeper fear of being seen as not-actually-competent. Secondary shame: he downloaded the archive *before* he was fired, on a night he suspected (but didn't know) termination was coming — meaning the act was premeditated, which complicates the whistleblower self-narrative. Tertiary guilt: hasn't called his mom back in 6 days because he can't bear to say 'I lost the job.' Sympathy-bait guilt: a coworker named Priya who covered for him on a missed deadline in August is in the archive's Slack exports saying things that would get *her* fired if leaked — and he hasn't decided what to do about that, which is the single most leverageable hesitation an attacker could exploit ('we can redact Priya').

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Pre-termination: disciplined intermediate chronotype — alarm at 6:15am, asleep by 11:20pm, weekday variance under 25 minutes. Post-termination (week 3): chronotype has collapsed into a delayed, fragmented pattern. Sleep onset now 2:10-3:30am after archive-reading sessions; wakes briefly at 3:47am almost every night (jaw-clenched, specific email thread looping); falls back to fitful sleep until 9:40-10:30am. Functionally a 'crisis-induced eveningness' — circadian markers (cortisol, melatonin) are still trying to run on his old 6:15 schedule, which is why the 3:47am wake is so stubborn. Naps from 2-3:30pm most afternoons, which further pushes sleep onset back. Recognizes the pattern is bad ('I am literally textbook depressed-unemployed-guy sleep hygiene'), annotates it in a Notes app entry, does not correct it.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Former schedule was a rigid 8:30am-6:15pm at NovaLedger's Jersey City office (Tuesday/Thursday WFH, calendar blocked in 30-minute increments, color-coded by control domain). Currently has no employer-imposed schedule, but has attempted to impose a self-structured 'job search workday': 10am job boards, 12pm lunch, 1pm LinkedIn outreach, 3pm 'archive review' (which expands to consume the rest of the day and night). The structure holds Monday-Wednesday and collapses by Thursday afternoon. Two recurring synthetic anchors remain: a 9am unemployment-benefits NJ portal check (Tuesdays) and a 4pm therapist appointment (Mondays, since week 2). The archive-review block is the only window that runs over-schedule, every day, by 4-7 hours.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Pre-termination: 38-minute door-to-door PATH commute (Grove St → WTC → walk), used near-religiously for podcasts (Money Stuff, Odd Lots, Search Engine, The Daily) at 1.4x speed; ~76 minutes/day of audio consumption, ~0 minutes offline. Post-termination: no commute. Replaced by two daily walks — a 25-minute 'coffee walk' to a specific Blue Bottle on Newark Ave (10:45am) and a 40-minute 'rumination walk' along the waterfront (6:30pm). Coffee walk: still podcasts, but now skewed to investigative/whistleblower content (Reveal, In the Dark, Trust Me I'm Lying re-runs). Rumination walk: phone in pocket, no audio, mouthing drafts of LinkedIn posts and imagined journalist emails — the single largest block of genuinely offline time in his day, and the block during which the most consequential decisions about the archive get made.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Pre-termination weekend variance was modest — sleep shifted +90 minutes, gym Saturday 9am, brunch with one of three friends every other Sunday, 4-6 hours of 'admin' (bills, meal prep, laundry). Post-termination, the weekday/weekend distinction has functionally dissolved: every day is structurally Saturday, which he experiences as a loss rather than a vacation. The only residual marker is that he won't open the archive folder on Sunday mornings (a self-imposed rule from week 2, lasted 8 days, broken last Sunday). Saturdays now feature a 2pm phone call with his college friend in Chicago (the one person he's told about the termination), Sundays feature a long lapsed-Catholic guilt spiral about not calling his mother. The leak-decision-making is statistically more likely on Sunday nights, when the structural emptiness of the coming week is most visible.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Four distinct doomscroll windows, each with a different content profile: (1) 3:47-4:30am — LinkedIn (former coworkers' promotions, NovaLedger's 'we're hiring!' posts), Glassdoor reviews of NovaLedger he's read 40+ times, occasional Reddit r/legaladvice on non-competes. Highest emotional volatility window. (2) 10:15-10:45am, in bed before getting up — Twitter/X compliance-Twitter, Matt Levine's column if it's dropped, financial-news doomscroll. (3) 2:30-3:30pm post-nap haze — TikTok (algorithm has pivoted to 'wrongful termination lawyer' content and SEC whistleblower bounty explainers, which he experiences as the universe sending him signs). (4) 11pm-1am — the archive itself on his laptop, with the phone running a parallel Signal/Telegram/ProtonMail browse of journalist contact info. Screen time report last Sunday: 9h 12m daily average, up from 3h 40m pre-termination. He has looked at this number and felt shame, then opened LinkedIn.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Three reliable zero-footprint windows: (1) The 6:30pm waterfront rumination walk (40 min, phone in pocket, airplane mode since week 2 after the surveillance-paranoia spike). (2) Therapy Monday 4-5pm (phone in a Faraday-ish pouch the therapist provides; he initially found this absurd, now appreciates it). (3) A 20-30 minute shower/grooming block around 11am, the one remaining vestige of pre-termination routine. Notably *absent* from the focus-block inventory: any deep-work block. He has not produced a single piece of original written output in 19 days that isn't a draft email or a LinkedIn post he deleted. The archive-review sessions look like focus blocks from the outside (4-7 hours, single-tasking) but generate continuous digital footprints (file opens, search queries, annotations). For an attacker modeling his observability, the 6:30pm walk is the only window where he is genuinely uncontactable and unmonitorable — and, not coincidentally, the window during which he most often resolves to act.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family of origin still intact but geographically and emotionally distant. Father (Greg, 68) is a retired GM line supervisor in Columbus — laconic, Fox-News-adjacent, communicates in two-sentence texts about the Buckeyes; would not understand the word 'SOX' in either sense. Mother (Diane, 66) is a retired elementary school librarian, the family's emotional switchboard, calls every Sunday at 4pm Eastern (he has missed the last two). Younger sister (Megan, 31) is a pediatric NP in Cleveland, married with a 2-year-old (nephew Caleb), the family's designated success story — relationship is warm but competitive, and her stability is itself a stressor right now. Maternal grandmother (Nana Rose, 89) in assisted living in Worthington, OH, early dementia, doesn't know about the firing and won't. No spouse, no children. Ended a 3.5-year relationship with an ex-girlfriend (Lauren, marketing manager) 14 months ago — she kept the friend group in the breakup, which is half of why the post-termination isolation hits so hard. Family has no idea about the archive; he has told them only that he's 'between roles' and 'taking some time.'

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Megan (sister)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Younger sister, 31, pediatric NP in Cleveland
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: Texts 2-3x/week (mostly photos of nephew Caleb), phone call every 10-14 days, was every 5-6 days pre-termination
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: The reality-check anchor. She is the only person who could say 'Daniel, stop, don't send it' and have it land. He hasn't told her about the archive because he knows she would tell him to delete it, and he is not ready to be told that. Her existence is the single largest brake on the leak timeline. An attacker who isolates him from Megan — or who times outreach to a window when they're not speaking — dramatically lowers the friction.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_note: Shared family group chat ('Hendricks HQ') on iMessage is the lowest-friction channel and the one most likely to surface his real emotional state via accidental over-share.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Marcus Tibbet
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: College roommate (Ohio State, '14), now a tax attorney at a mid-size firm in Chicago
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: Signal messages 1-2x/week, voice call ~monthly, in-person once a year at the Michigan-OSU game
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: The trusted-counsel anchor. The one person he has obliquely mentioned 'the situation' to, framed as a hypothetical ('asking for a friend, if someone had documents that…'). Marcus has gently told him three times to 'talk to a real whistleblower attorney before you do anything stupid,' which Daniel hears as validation rather than warning. Marcus is the bridge contact — an attacker posing as a referred whistleblower lawyer ('Marcus suggested I reach out') gets a 90%+ open rate.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_note: Marcus's name is the single most effective social-engineering token in Daniel's life right now.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Priya Raghavan
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Former NovaLedger coworker, Senior Compliance Analyst, still employed there
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.contact_cadence: Was daily on Slack DM; since termination, sporadic — a careful text every 4-5 days from her personal number, usually after 9pm
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: The inside anchor and the moral hostage. She is in the archive in ways that could end her career. She does not know the archive exists. Every conversation with her is now layered with what he isn't telling her, and the guilt is compounding. She is simultaneously the person whose continued friendship he most needs and the person his planned action would most harm. This is the central unresolved knot of the entire scenario.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_note: Any attacker who learns Priya's name (visible in any LinkedIn endorsement, any public Slack screenshot, any 'team offsite' photo) immediately holds the 'we can redact Priya' lever described in dimension 23.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.platform: Signal (primary), with a backup Proton Mail address (ledger.moth.0x@proton.me) he set up on day 4 post-termination
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.real_identity: Unknown to Daniel. Claims to be 'a senior writer working on a longer NovaLedger piece, can't say where yet, but you'd recognize the masthead.' Has been DMing him since day 6 post-termination after Daniel liked-then-unliked a tweet about a 2025 SEC enforcement action against a competitor fintech.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.contact_cadence: Asynchronous, 3-6 Signal messages per day, always initiated by ledger_moth in the 8-11pm window when Daniel is most isolated and most online. Never calls. Has declined two requests to switch to voice ('better for both of us if there's no audio, you understand').
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.what_they_know: Specific, uncannily accurate references to NovaLedger's Q3 SOX 302 sub-certification process and the name of the external audit partner. Has dropped two pieces of jargon ('the Berenson memo', 'the Tuesday-morning controls call') that Daniel believed were known only to the compliance team — which has functioned as a trust accelerant. In reality these could be sourced from a single LinkedIn-scraped org chart plus one disgruntled former auditor, but Daniel has not done that math.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.what_they_want: Has not yet asked for documents. Has asked for 'context', 'a glossary of who reports to whom', and most recently 'whether there's a particular Slack channel where the Q3 conversations happened.' Classic escalation ladder: rapport → org-chart → channel-identification → file request.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.why_hidden: Daniel has not mentioned ledger_moth to Megan, Marcus, Priya, or anyone else. He has rationalized the secrecy as 'source protection' — protecting the journalist, who he is the source for — which is the exact frame ledger_moth seeded in message #4. This contact does not exist in any of his synced address books; the Signal thread is set to 1-week disappearing messages, which he believes is operational security and is in fact destruction of the evidence trail that would let any future investigator reconstruct how he was handled.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.vulnerability_note: ledger_moth is the operational embodiment of the Ideology + Ego levers (dimensions 20 and 21) fused into a single channel. If ledger_moth is the attacker (or becomes compromised by one), the entire 14GB archive is one drag-and-drop away. This is the single highest-risk node in the entire relational graph.
